title: [proof-gap] Theorem 1 (thm:main) stated under (H1)-(H3) but its proof requires the undeclared lifting hypothesis (H4)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Type:** logical-scope mismatch (hypothesis used but not in the theorem's hypotheses).

**Status vs. history:** This is the still-open residual of the prior critic's Gap 5. The solver reported "(H4) is now stated, with verification in the loop/multiloop cases." That is true of the *proof text*, but **Theorem 1 itself still reads "Assume (H1)-(H3). Then Aut(M^Gamma)=E(X,g)^Gamma."** The proof of Lemma `lem:varietypart` then *introduces* (H4) as a new "standing hypothesis" and concedes the lift "is *not* automatic for a general Galois cover ... there is in general an obstruction (a class in H^1)."

**Precise defect.** The group E(X,g)^Gamma and the induced operator Phi f(x)=Psi(x)(f(phi^{-1}x)) are defined via a genuine variety automorphism phi of X. The reconstruction from an arbitrary Lie automorphism produces only bar-phi on X/Gamma (via the centroid C(M^Gamma)=A^Gamma). Lifting bar-phi to a Gamma-normalizing phi on X needs (H4). Hence the equality Aut(M^Gamma)=E(X,g)^Gamma is unproven under (H1)-(H3) alone; the "broad generality" headline over-claims.

**Required fix (either):**
1. Add (H4) to the hypotheses of Theorem 1 (and justify it where invoked: loop/multiloop give explicit lifts; for X/Gamma a torus cite Pianzola's H^1-vanishing with the precise statement and verify its hypotheses); OR
2. Restate the conclusion intrinsically over X/Gamma as an exact sequence
   1 -> G_Gamma -> Aut(M^Gamma) -> Aut(X/Gamma)_lift -> 1,
   where the gauge group is the group of regular sections of the descended (twisted) group scheme underline-G=(pi_* G_X)^Gamma over X/Gamma and Aut(X/Gamma)_lift is the subgroup that lifts, with the lifting obstruction made explicit. Then nothing is over-claimed.
